mnemonics: TEPE: Think of a well-posed problem as a TEPE (Task, Experience, Performance measure) that needs to be clearly defined before learning can begin.
keyPoints: A well-defined learning problem must specify a Task, T.
It must specify a class of Experiences, E, from which the system will learn.
It must have a quantitative Performance measure, P, to evaluate the solution.
commonMistakes: Confusing the performance measure (P) with the task itself (T).
Assuming any experience (E) is sufficient, rather than requiring a specific, relevant class of data.
